subject: Project Management
title: Total Quality Management
description: A fast breakdown of Total Quality Management for builders.

hook.text: In the 1980s, American companies panicked about Japanese quality and embraced Total Quality Management.

buildup.text: TQM combined Deming's, Juran's, and Crosby's ideas into a company-wide quality philosophy.

discovery.text: TQM meant everyone — from CEO to janitor — was responsible for quality. It wasn't just the quality department's job.

twist.text: Many companies adopted TQM as a buzzword without the discipline. By the mid-1990s, TQM fatigue had set in.

climax.text: TQM faded as a brand, but its principles live on in every lean and Six Sigma program today.

punchline.text: The movement died. Its ideas are everywhere.

quiz.question: What happened to Total Quality Management?
options: It faded as a brand, but its principles live on in lean and Six Sigma
It replaced all other quality methods
It was proven to be ineffective
